# BitMono

> Free & open-source obfuscator for .NET, Mono and Unity — obfuscate an assembly right in the browser at bitmono.dev, or via CLI, NuGet/MSBuild, Unity packages and GitHub Actions. The site also hosts a gallery of BitMono-obfuscated reverse-engineering challenges (crackmes) with writeups and a leaderboard.

## Key pages

- [Obfuscator](https://bitmono.dev/): drop a .dll/.exe, pick protections, download it obfuscated — nothing is stored
- [Download](https://bitmono.dev/download): CLI, Unity package and NuGet builds for every runtime
- [Crackmes](https://bitmono.dev/crackmes): downloadable .NET reverse-engineering challenges
- [Blog](https://bitmono.dev/blog): notes on .NET obfuscation and reverse engineering
- [Blog RSS](https://bitmono.dev/blog/rss.xml): full-text feed of new posts
- [FAQ](https://bitmono.dev/faq): how the obfuscator and the crackmes gallery work

## Documentation & source

- [Docs](https://docs.bitmono.dev): every protection, configuration option and integration guide
- [Engine on GitHub](https://github.com/bitmono-project/BitMono): MIT-licensed source
- [Sitemap](https://bitmono.dev/sitemap.xml)
